controls.control_objective: To ensure be a customer of the ad bank, b) signed contract / invoice is in a freely convertible currency, c) the beneficiary is willing to receive the payment i
controls.control_description:
(vi) 13Settlement of Export transactions in currencies not having a direct exchange rate
To further liberalise the procedure and facilitate settlement of export transactions where the
invoicing is in a freely convertible currency and the settlement takes place in the currency of
the beneficiary, which though convertible, does not have a direct exchange rate, it has been
decided that AD Category-I banks may permit settlement of such export transactions
(excluding those put through the ACU mechanism), subject to conditions as under:
a) Exporter shall be a customer of the AD Bank,
b) Signed contract / invoice is in a freely convertible currency,
c) The beneficiary is willing to receive the payment in the currency of beneficiary instead of
the original (freely convertible) currency of the invoice/ contract, Letter of Credit as full and
final settlement,
d) AD bank is satisfied with the bonafides of the transactions, and
e) The counterparty to the exporter/ importer of the AD bank is not from a country or
jurisdiction in the updated FATF Public Statement on High Risk & Non Co-operative
Jurisdictions on which FATF has called for counter measures.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be a customer of the ad bank, b) signed contract / invoice is in a freely convertible currency, c) the beneficiary is willing to receive the payment i

controls.control_objective: To ensure the realisation and repatriation requirements as specified in regulation 9 of foreign exchange management (export of goods and services) regulations
controls.control_description:
Funds in this account may be utilised by the exporter for paying for its imports into India or repatriated into India within a period not exceeding the end of the next month from the date of receipt of the funds after adjusting for forward commitments, provided that the realisation and repatriation requirements as specified in Regulation 9 of Foreign Exchange Management (Export of Goods and Services) Regulations, 2015 are also met.'

controls.control_objective: To ensure be permitted against the security of balances held in eefc accounts by the ad category - i banks

security of balances held in EEFC accounts by the AD Category - I banks.

controls.control_objective: To ensure be converted into rupees on or before the last day of the succeeding calendar month after adjusting for utilization of the balances for approved purpo
controls.control_description:
(iv) All categories of foreign exchange earners are allowed to credit 100% of their foreign
exchange earnings to their EEFC Accounts subject to the condition that
a) The sum total of the accruals in the account during a calendar month should be converted
into Rupees on or before the last day of the succeeding calendar month after adjusting for
utilization of the balances for approved purposes or forward commitments.

at the applicable rate.

concerned of the Reserve Bank.

controls.control_description:

has to pass before crossing over to the foreign territory.

controls.control_description:

arrange either to give the form to the person in charge of the vessel or vehicle or forward it to

controls.control_objective: To ensure move straight on to the foreign country without further formalities at the border
controls.control_description:
They will collect the EDF for goods loaded at
these stations so that the goods may move straight on to the foreign country without further
formalities at the border.

controls.control_objective: To ensure arrange to present edf to the customs officer at the border land customs station where customs formalities are completed
controls.control_description:
For goods loaded at stations other than the designated stations, exporters must
arrange to present EDF to the Customs Officer at the Border Land Customs Station where
Customs formalities are completed.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to arrange to present edf to the customs officer at the border land customs station where customs formalities are completed

controls.control_objective: To ensure be settled in any permitted currency in addition to the asian clearing union mechanism
controls.control_description:
Accordingly, all trade
transactions with Myanmar, including those at the Indo-Myanmar border with effect from
December 1, 2015 shall be settled in any permitted currency in addition to the Asian Clearing
Union mechanism.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be settled in any permitted currency in addition to the asian clearing union mechanism

controls.control_objective: To ensure utilize the funds for import of goods from romania into india within six months from the date of credit to escrow accounts allowed to be opened
controls.control_description:
A.10 Counter -Trade arrangements with Romania
The Reserve Bank will consider counter trade proposals from Indian exporters with Romania
involving adjustment of value of exports from India against value of imports made into India in
terms of a voluntarily entered arrangement between the concerned parties, subject to the
condition, among others that the Indian exporter should utilize the funds for import of goods
from Romania into India within six months from the date of credit to Escrow Accounts allowed
to be opened.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to utilize the funds for import of goods from romania into india within six months from the date of credit to escrow accounts allowed to be opened

controls.control_objective: To ensure be done through an ad bank
controls.control_description:
Remittance of commitment fee / service charges, etc.,
payable by the exporter as approved by the EXIM Bank / AD Category - I banks concerned
may be done through an AD bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be done through an ad bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be made in advance in one lump sum or at monthly intervals as approved by the authority concerned
controls.control_description:
Such remittances may be made in advance in one lump
sum or at monthly intervals as approved by the authority concerned.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be made in advance in one lump sum or at monthly intervals as approved by the authority concerned

controls.control_objective: To ensure take their own business decision to enter into export factoring arrangement on non-recourse basis

controls.control_description:
The
invoices purchased should represent genuine trade invoices.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to represent genuine trade invoices

controls.control_objective: To ensure pass on the net value to the financing bank/ institution after realising the export proceeds
controls.control_description:

may pass on the net value to the financing bank/ Institution after realising the export
proceeds.

controls.control_description:

controls.control_objective: To ensure close the export bills and report the same in the export data processing and monitoring system (edpms) of the reserve bank of india
controls.control_description:
(v) After factoring, the Export Factor may close the export bills and report the same in the
Export Data Processing and Monitoring System (EDPMS) of the Reserve Bank of
India.

controls.control_objective: To ensure obtain credit evaluation details from the correspondent bank abroad
controls.control_description:
(vi) In case of single factor, not involving Import Factor overseas, the Export Factor may
obtain credit evaluation details from the correspondent bank abroad.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to obtain credit evaluation details from the correspondent bank abroad

controls.control_objective: To ensure be ensured by the export factor
controls.control_description: (vii) KYC and due diligence on the exporter shall be ensured by the Export Factor.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be ensured by the export factor

controls.control_description:

exporters are required to obtain the approval of the AD Category - I banks/ Exim Bank at

controls.control_description:

controls.control_objective: To ensure deploy their temporary cash surpluses, generated outside india investments in short-term paper abroad including treasury bills and other monetary inst
controls.control_description:
c) Deployment of temporary cash surpluses - Subject to monitoring by the AD Category - I
bank(s) / Exim Bank, Project / Service exporters may deploy their temporary cash surpluses,
generated outside India investments in short-term paper abroad including treasury bills and
other monetary instruments with a maturity or remaining maturity of one year or less and the
rating of which should be at least A-1/AAA by Standard & Poor or P-1/-AAA by Moody's or
F1/AAA by Fitch IBCA etc., and as deposits with branches / subsidiaries outside India of AD
Category - I banks in India.

also not a traveler coming from and going to Pakistan or Bangladesh, and visiting India may

to an amount not exceeding Rs.

shall be retained by ADs and
may not be submitted to the Reserve Bank.
